title: Pycnospora lutescens

herb_names_common.name: yellow pycnospora is a common name for this species

uses: Pycnospora lutescens is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in West Africa, where it is employed to treat various ailments. The plant's extracts have been found to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin infections. Traditionally, the plant is used to treat fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. Its roots are used in folk medicine to treat stomach problems and other health conditions. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and potential side effects of using Pycnospora lutescens for medicinal purposes.
taxonomy: Pycnospora lutescens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions of the world, specifically in Southeast Asia. It is a part of the genus Pycnospora, which is a relatively small group of plants. The classification of Pycnospora lutescens is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta. It is also known to be a member of the order Sapindales.
